step_by_step_thinking:
The type of bur used during periradicular surgery, especially for precise removal or shaping of bony structures around the roots, requires sharp precision to avoid damage. A round bur would be too blunt for such tasks while fissure and taper burs lack sufficient size for effective use on periapical areas. The double-inverted cone bur provides both cutting efficiency and control needed for this delicate procedure.

raw_response:
The type of bur used during periradicular surgery, especially for precise removal or shaping of bony structures around the roots, requires sharp precision to avoid damage. A round bur would be too blunt for such tasks while fissure and taper burs lack sufficient size for effective use on periapical areas. The double-inverted cone bur provides both cutting efficiency and control needed for this delicate procedure.
